title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 15
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your local art gallery is planning to modernise its permanent exhibition. You have been asked to write a report for the curator suggesting how to use technology to make the art more accessible to younger visitors.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report: Digital Modernisation of the City Art Gallery

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to recommend technological integrations for the upcoming renovation of our permanent exhibition. The goal is to enhance engagement among visitors aged 16–25, a demographic that is currently underrepresented.

Augmented Reality (AR) Integration
The most effective way to engage younger visitors is through the use of Augmented Reality. By developing a dedicated gallery app, we can allow visitors to point their smartphones at specific paintings to see 'hidden' layers, such as the artist’s initial sketches or historical videos explaining the context of the work. This turns a passive viewing experience into an interactive journey of discovery.

Interactive Digital Workshops
I also suggest replacing one of the smaller galleries with a 'Digital Creation Suite'. Here, visitors could use touch-screen tablets to try their hand at digital painting or photo manipulation, perhaps recreating the styles of the masters featured in the exhibition. This practical element would bridge the gap between historical art and modern digital creativity.

Social Connectivity
Finally, the gallery should install 'digital photo points'—aesthetically pleasing areas designed for social media sharing, with QR codes that link directly to the history of the nearby artwork. This encourages visitors to become digital ambassadors for the gallery.

Conclusion
Younger audiences do not want to just look at art; they want to interact with it. By implementing these digital tools, the gallery can remain a relevant cultural hub for the next generation.

tasks.title: Email/Letter
tasks.prompt: You are interested in attending an expensive professional development course, and you want your employer to pay for it. Write an email to your manager explaining the benefits of the course for the company and how it will improve your performance.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. Grayson,

I am writing to propose my attendance at the 'Advanced Strategic Leadership' course, which is being held in Berlin this coming October. After reviewing the curriculum, I am convinced that the skills taught would be of significant benefit to our upcoming project expansions.

The course focuses specifically on managing remote, international teams and implementing lean project management methodologies. As we are planning to scale our operations in the Asian market next year, the insights I would gain into cross-cultural leadership and crisis management would be directly applicable to my role as Team Lead. Specifically, I believe the modules on 'Agile Workflow' would help us reduce our current project delivery times by optimizing our internal communication processes.

I understand that the enrolment fee is substantial. However, I view this as a strategic investment in our department’s efficiency. Upon my return, I would be prepared to lead a series of internal workshops to share the core principles and materials with the rest of the management team, ensuring that the knowledge is disseminated throughout the company.

I have attached the full course brochure and a breakdown of the costs for your consideration. I truly believe that the value I will bring back to the firm far outweighs the initial expense. 

I would be happy to discuss this with you further during our weekly catch-up on Thursday.

Best regards,

Sara Miller

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement on a book website: 'We want reviews of biographies or autobiographies of people who have achieved something extraordinary. Tell us about the person, the challenges they faced, and what you learned from their story.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Beyond the Stars: A Review of 'Becoming' by Michelle Obama

In her candid and deeply moving autobiography, 'Becoming', Michelle Obama invites readers into the private world of a woman who has navigated some of the most powerful and scrutinised spaces on earth. It is far more than a political memoir; it is a profound exploration of identity and purpose.

The book traces her journey from the modest South Side of Chicago to the White House. What makes her story so compelling are the challenges she faced as a woman of colour in high-achieving environments, long before she became First Lady. Her descriptions of 'imposter syndrome' and the constant struggle to balance her professional ambitions with motherhood are handled with refreshing honesty. She does not shy away from the sacrifices required to support her husband's career while trying to maintain her own sense of self.

What I learned most from her story is the importance of 'owning' one's narrative. She emphasizes that our backgrounds—however humble—are our strengths, not our weaknesses. Her resilience in the face of public criticism and her commitment to using her platform for social good are truly inspirational.

I would recommend this book to anyone, regardless of their political leanings. It is a beautifully written testament to the power of education, hard work, and staying true to one's values. It leaves the reader feeling that they, too, have the potential to become something extraordinary.
